episodes.title: Les Wexner repeatedly denies knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein's sex crimes under oath
episodes.description:
Columbus billionaire Les Wexner denied under oath that he ever saw Jeffrey Epstein engage in sexual contact with underage girls or young women, saying he was unaware of Epstein’s conduct while Epstein managed his finances.

The House Oversight Committee released video of Wexner’s deposition this week after questioning him at his New Albany home.

He described their relationship as professional and repeatedly answered “absolutely not” when asked if he saw sexual contact or other criminal activity.

Wexner also testified he did not recall ever seeing photographs or other images of women or minors during any visits to Epstein’s properties.

He told lawmakers he never traveled on Epstein’s plane and said he visited Epstein’s island once, staying about an hour during what he described as a family cruise. Asked whether he recalled seeing any prominent figures there, Wexner replied, “No. It was a pretty crummy island.”

Wexner acknowledged one trip to Epstein’s ranch in New Mexico, saying he and his wife, Abigail, flew down, drove around for “an hour or two,” then came home. Wexner said he saw no one notable at the property.

The testimony came as state lawmakers reopened an investigation into the ranch, looking into allegations of potential criminal activity after the DOJ released FBI records. Oversight Committee members pressed Wexner on the brevity of those trips. He answered that quick travel was routine for him, comparing getting on a plane to “going to the mailbox.”

Wexner also faced questions about a birthday note he contributed for Epstein’s 50th birthday. The letter included the line, “I wanted to get you what you want so here it is.” Asked what he believed Epstein would want, Wexner said Epstein was a bachelor and that he drew “a pair of boobs” as an offhand joke.

A longtime Epstein associate, Ghislaine Maxwell, previously told the DOJ that Epstein ran New Albany. Wexner disputed that, saying Epstein was a financial adviser to the New Albany Company.

When asked about Epstein's title as co-president, Wexner said he did not remember, adding, "[I] was the president, chairman of the board, and then the CEO of a company. But I really just thought my job was to be the president. So that the titles wouldn't have meant anything to me."

Wexner denied knowing about quarterly payments from Epstein going to OSU Wexner Medical Center OB-GYN, Dr. Mark Landon, who has said he was paid for consulting work related to biotech investments.

MORE | 'It's time for change,' nurses call for removal of Wexner, Landon names at OSUMC
Wexner, whose wife and daughter received services from the OB-GYN, called it “weird” that money tied to him was used for consulting services without his knowledge. He said Landon never talked to him about the payments, and Wexner did not know Epstein knew the doctor.

After answering questions for nearly four and a half hours, a microphone picked up one of Wexner’s lawyers urging him to shorten his responses. In the recording, the attorney said, “I will f****ng kill you if you answer another question with more than five words,” prompting laughter from Wexner before the lawyer told him to answer the question. The deposition continued for about 30 more minutes.

Wexner defended his character to the end, saying rumors and innuendos made him angry because he considered himself “a very straight arrow” and “a very responsible person.” He also said he now believes Epstein lived a double life and that, looking back, he had been conned by what he called a “world Olympic all-time con artist.”
_______________

episodes.title: Les Wexner deposition on Epstein relationship released by House Oversight Committee
episodes.description:
The full video showing Columbus billionaire Les Wexner's deposition before a House Oversight panel was released Thursday afternoon.

The video is nearly five hours long and shows the entirety of Wexner's testimony that took place at his New Albany compound on Wednesday.

Wexner, the founder of L Brands, is mentioned repeatedly in the Jeffrey Epstein files. A Wexner spokesperson said after the deposition that Wexner "honestly answered every question put to him today by the Committee," and reiterated that he "has no knowledge of, and did not participate in, Epstein’s illegal conduct."

Epstein, the disgraced financier, died in federal custody in 2019 while awaiting trial on federal sex-trafficking charges.

During a midday break Wednesday, several of the committee members updated reporters on the progress of the deposition.

"There is no single person that was more involved in providing Jeffrey Epstein with the financial support to commit his crimes than Les Wexner," Rep. Robert Garcia, D-Calif., said, adding that survivors have raised concerns about the scale of money transfers connected to Wexner and Epstein.

episodes.title: Democrats grill Les Wexner on Jeffrey Epstein ties; Wexner calls Epstein a "world-class con man"
episodes.description:
Lawmakers questioned Columbus billionaire Les Wexner on Wednesday at his home in New Albany about his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein, who died in federal custody in 2019 while awaiting trial on federal sex-trafficking charges.

Wexner, the founder of L Brands, is mentioned repeatedly in the Epstein files. A spokesperson said after the deposition that Wexner "honestly answered every question put to him today by the Committee," and reiterated that he "has no knowledge of, and did not participate in, Epstein’s illegal conduct."

Rep. Robert Garcia argued Wexner played a central role in financing Epstein. "There is no single person that was more involved in providing Jeffrey Epstein with the financial support to commit his crimes than Les Wexner," Garcia told reporters. He said survivors have raised concerns about the scale of money transfers connected to Wexner and Epstein.

Garcia pointed to what he described as a billion dollars in value moving to Epstein. "To be very clear, we know that approximately over a billion dollars, it looks like—it's about a billion dollars—that was either transferred, provided in stocks, or given directly to Mr. Epstein by Wexner," he said.

Garcia said Wexner claimed he was unaware of how much money moved between them and was trying to minimize how close the two men were over roughly two decades.

“We should be very clear that there would be no Epstein island, there would be no Epstein plane, there would be no money to traffic women and girls, Mr. Epstein would not be the wealthy man he was without the support of Les Wexner,” Garcia said.

Wexner reportedly told the committee he was never on Epstein’s plane and visited Epstein’s island only once during what he described as a family cruise on their boat. Rep. Yassamin Ansari of Arizona said she questioned him about that trip.

“He claimed he was on the island for one hour, and that he walked around with his family. I asked if any other individuals were there and he said no,” Ansari said.

Ahead of the deposition, Wexner—largely out of the public eye in recent years—issued a rare statement calling Epstein a "world-class con man" and saying he cut ties with Epstein nearly 20 years ago.

Wexner said he met Epstein in the “mid-to-late 1980s” and eventually hired him to manage his personal finances at the recommendation of other businessmen. He said he gave Epstein “power of attorney” to manage transactions quickly.

“What is so clear only now is that Epstein lived a double life,” Wexner said, adding, “At no time did I ever witness the side of Epstein’s life for which he is now infamous.”

Wexner said that in 2007, as Epstein was accused of soliciting prostitution in Florida, a review of his finances revealed “Epstein had stolen vast sums from our family.” Wexner said, “Epstein agreed to, and did, return a substantial amount of money.” He added, “In light of his eventual guilty plea and deception of our family, we completely severed our relationship with Epstein.”

Wexner also addressed questions about his travel and contact with Epstein, saying, “I was never on his airplane,” and that he “visited Epstein’s island only once” with his family during a cruise on their boat. He said he never saw or heard about Epstein being in the company of a minor girl.

“I am the father of two girls, now women, and the thought of what he did makes me sick,” Wexner said. He also addressed Epstein’s survivors, saying, “The pain he inflicted upon them is unfathomable to me. My heart goes out to each of them.”

In the Epstein files, a 2019 FBI document alleges Wexner was a co-conspirator. Wexner denied that, saying, “I was never a participant nor co-conspirator in any of Epstein’s illegal activities.”

Wexner’s statement does not directly mention prominent Epstein victim Virginia Giuffre, who claimed Epstein trafficked her to Wexner. Wexner said any suggestion he was ever unfaithful to his wife is “absolutely and entirely false,” and described “enormous embarrassment and regret,” saying he was “duped by a world-class con man.”
_______________

episodes.title: FULL: Clintons refuse to testify in House Epstein probe, Comer threatens contempt charges
episodes.description:
Bill and Hillary Clinton declined to testify Tuesday before the Republican-led House Oversight Committee in its Jeffrey Epstein investigation, instead issuing a forceful letter to Chairman James Comer defending their refusal.

“Every person has to decide when they have seen or had enough and are ready to fight for this country, its principles and its people, no matter the consequences,” the Clintons wrote in the lengthy letter. “For us, now is that time.”

Comer threatened to initiate contempt proceedings against the Clintons in December if they failed to appear for depositions and sent a letter to the couple's attorney, demanding they attend.

Read more: https://abc6onyourside.com/news/nation-world/bill-hillary-clinton-refuse-to-testify-in-house-jeffrey-epstein-files-probe-as-house-oversight-committee-chairman-james-comer-threatens-contempt-charges-justice-department
